The normal map does not look correct in MakeHuman viewport but work in Blender. Of some reason it appears inverted with the 'None' topologie but correct with the 'Adult male genitalia' topologie. The original textures are 8192x8192 therefor some edges may be visible but I have tried to correct them. The normal map is quite strong and looks better when not set to 1 eg '0.7'. The preview image is how the skin looks direct after import in Blender Internal without specular map. For all audience I let the character wear swimming trunks. 2018-02-13 Updated the textures.

skins.name: trashhunter_black_kameri_fur

skins.description: The Kameri are a humaonid species with black and white striped fur which jokingly call themselves two-legged zebra. Usually black dominates the coat pattern and the stripes are white. Very rarely there is a variant in which white dominates as the basic color. This is the usual black dominated coat. Important: Kameri are hermaphrodite and always appear in female appearance. To reproduce a Kameri can turn her vagina inside out and reshape it into a penis to fertilize her mate. So please don't anyone come up with the idea of giving birth to male Kameri. They do not exist.

The problems I mentioned in the thread too many overlap overcomplicated mesh etc are still there but I thought might as well share what I have got. Who knows maybe somebody will use it for something or maybe it will give somebody new ideas. There's still not a lot of usable hair in MH repository so I guess every little bit helps

hair.name: o4saken_chinesebob01
hair.author: punkduck
hair.description: The real author of this asset is o4saken ... I only changed a few vertices and added a new 'base scalp' then I created textures with Blender strands on a transparent background . For the material: best to plugin the color input of the glossy shader to the output of the diffuse texture in cycles. The texture is identically to the O4saken curly01 version. If you allready loaded the hair 'O4saken curly01' and want to save a few mbytes on the disk don't load the textures and edit the .mhmat file instead change texture pathes to hair/ o4saken_curly_dir /o4saken_curly01.png ...

hair.name: o4saken_curly01
hair.author: punkduck
hair.description: The real author of this asset is o4saken ... I only changed a few vertices and added a new 'base scalp' then I created textures with Blender strands on a transparent background . Be aware that the export of this asset to Blender will fail until the exporter is fixed. The reason is the number of vertices 100000. I will add a text to the 'unofficial plugins' folder with a workaround. For the material: best to plugin the color input of the glossy shader to the output of the diffuse texture in cycles.

clothes.name: aethelraed_flapper_dress
clothes.author: Aethelraed_Unraed
clothes.description: 20s inspired dress. Made this for a project of mine and releasing it to the public on what happens to be the day of the Women's March against Trump. What coincidence Warning: mesh uses both tris and quads. The current release of MakeHuman doesn't seem to have a problem with it: it shows up correctly and exports correctly to Blender including rigging. It may not work for everyone's system/MH release though so use with caution. It may export incorrectly crash MakeHuman fry your computer or release the nuclear codes to a bloke called Donald. Oh wait that last one's already been done nevermind Known issues: - Using Blender Subsurf modifier after importing from mhx2 may crash Blender or cause other issues. Doing a degenerate dissolve on the dress after import seems to fix this issue although Blender says nothing happened . - As is the case with pretty much all dresses extreme poses may look awkward. Two additional materials are available at the materials page: http://www.makehumancommunity.org/materials.html. Attribution not required but always appreciated

clothes.name: drednicolson_asymmetric_tunic_and_sash
clothes.author: DredNicolson
clothes.description: A sleeveless woman's tunic with asymmetric seams and hem with a sash around the hips. Fitted on the female base human. The diffuse texture is derived from GIMP's built-in Wildflowers pattern and one of my really old procedural textures where I was attempting to make wood and not succeeding makes pretty good stripes though . The normal and specular maps come from a desaturated fabric photo and an old pattern where I literally modelled thread with lots and lots of tiny cylinders. A grayscale bump map was baked first then I used that to bake normals. Most skirts/pants/underwear should fit underneath with no or minor clipping and there's enough mesh density in the chest area to fit most ahem cup sizes. Known Issues - Minor clipping or odd deformations occur in front of the armpits when the shoulders are rolled you can see it on the Benchmark or other extreme arm poses . For now it's easy enough to hot-fix in Blender until I figure out which helper verts are the guilty party. Plans - Add a tie for the sash. This might be better done as a separate accessory.

clothes.name: drednicolson_pith_helmet
clothes.author: DredNicolson
clothes.description: A classic jungle explorer pith helmet. Fitted on the neutral base human. The cap area is actually separate from the brim with the cords hiding the empty space. This let me add more loops to the cap to make the seams while not having to add unneeded loops to the brim just to match topology. It fits over short hairstyles without issue but most long hair will clip through. You can hot-fix this in Blender with shape keys and/or a Mask modifier. Known Issues - Scales a little unevenly. You can see some minor squash and stretch when moving the Age slider. I tried several 3-vertex combos on the Hair helper for the rigid fit method and this is the best result of the bunch.

clothes.name: drednicolson_short-tail_camo_tee
clothes.author: DredNicolson
clothes.description: A casual shirt with the tail not quite reaching the hips fully modeled seams and a camouflage pattern. Fitted on the female base human. Works okay for males too albeit with a 'shrunk in the wash' look. The camo was created with procedural textures multiplied down with a light AO map and then baked to an image. The specular map emphasizes self-shadowing in the seams. The normal map simulates individual stitches to produce a fabric-like sheen. The effect is more like satin than cotton but close enough. The chest area readily adapts to standard breast targets though minor clipping can occur with extreme combinations. Known Issues - A bit of ugly deformation in the armpits. Can be hot-fixed in Blender with Subsurf and Corrective Smooth modifiers.

clothes.name: grinsegold_mars_suit
clothes.author: grinsegold
clothes.description: Gender slider set to full male recommended sorry sisters . Exporting unsubdivided Mesh recommended. Blender's Subdiv.-Surface-Modifier set to 3 better 4 recommended. The 'displacement texture' in fact is an emission map. Look here how to combine normal- and bumpmaps in Cycles: http://www.makehumancommunity.org/forum/viewtopic.php?f=14&t=14595 Edit: new vertex-triplet assigned to the rigid part. No more unwanted deformations. clothes.name: mindfront_ballet_dress_the_swan
clothes.author: Mindfront
clothes.description: Tags: female ballet work. I don't know anything about ballet but this is my/our interpretation of a ballet dress and was created together with a friend who did the basic design. It is primary made for subsurf level 2. I have done extra edge loops to tighten the edges to avoid texture distortion when using modifiers. The skirt is three vertex hard body to maintain its shape. The chest ruffle is not displaced and if you think the normal map is to strong you can reduce the effect by painting over the area with RGB color 128 128 256 in Blender 0.5 0.5 1.0 and low strength brush. In MakeHuman viewport the transparent will not show in favor of the normal map. In Blender use the modifier 'Solidify' and about 2 to 3 mm thickness and 'Fill Rim' and 'Only Rim' to give the fabric some thickness. In Blender Cycles: The diffuse texture should also be used as glossy color to make the gold pattern more visible. The specular map is also used to adjust glossy roughness use a 'Invert'-node and connect it to the glossy shader 'Roughness' to get tiny almost not noticeable glitter effect on the gold pattern.

clothes.name: mindfront_bikini_01
clothes.author: Mindfront
clothes.description: Tags: Female Swimwear. This bikini is also included in F Beach Dress 01. I have not made any effort in the material settings.

clothes.name: mindfront_cardigan_long_open_front
clothes.author: Mindfront
clothes.description: Tags: Female Sweater Cardigan. Long open-front cardigan. Loose knitted net fabric uses the alpha channel for transparency. Looks best with at least one level of subsurf. In Blender use the Solidify modifier with thickness about 3mm fill rim and only rim to give the fabric some thickness. The preview image is rendered in Blender Cycles and I used proportional edit and sculpt to adjust the cardigan to fit the character and pose.

clothes.name: mindfront_f_dress_01
clothes.author: Mindfront
clothes.description: Tags: Female Elegant. A Cocktail Dress was my intention looks more like a funeral outfit. The texture colors looks terrible in Blender Cycles I don't know why. One solution is to use the Gamma node and set it on something like 0.7 or 0.8 to adjust OR just change the colors to something better. Updated 2017-07-15 Better deformations than the previous version especially the skirt part.

clothes.name: mindfront_f_dress_02
clothes.author: Mindfront
clothes.description: Tags: Female Casual. A simple summer dress. Hard to make loose fitting clothes especially at the chest at least with my knowledge so far. The seams at the back above the shoulder blades will be deformed when subsurfed without extra edgeloops. If you use Blender use the Sculpt Mode with Clay and Smooth brush at low strength to adjust deformation and fitness. Updated 2017-07-15 Better deformations than the previous version especially the skirt part.

clothes.name: mindfront_f_dress_03
clothes.author: Mindfront
clothes.description: Tags: Female Elegant. My interpretation of a vintage weddingdress. I am experimenting on making more advanced clothes. Some areas may overlap each other especially on the skirt. I tried to make the belt buckle more static to avoid strong deformation. The flower pattern have some mismatching areas but it shouldn't be noticeably. If you use Blender use the Sculpt Mode with Clay and Smooth brush at low strength to adjust deformation and fitness. Updated 2017-07-15 The skirt part now use rigid groups to keep its form as I could not get it to deform smoothly otherwise.

clothes.name: mindfront_f_dress_04
clothes.author: Mindfront
clothes.description: Tags: Female Elegant. Not as much folds on this one as I thought of comfortable spandex like fabric. I made the buckle a hard body but in some cases it will loose its rectangular geometry. The diffuse texture and specular map looks the same but they are not. The specular map is specially made for Blender Cycles and should be set to 'Non-Color Data'. In Blender Cycles you can also use the specular map to get different glossy roughness between the fabric and the metal parts. Add a 'math'-node and set it to 'Multiply' and connect the color output of the specular map into the 'math'-node upper slot and set the value to eg 24 then add a 'Invert'-node and connect the 'Math'-node to the color input of the 'Invert-node' and the 'Invert'-node output to the glossy roughness input. The preview is how the dress should look in Blender Cycles after above settings. Updated 2017-07-15 Made new vertex binding to get some better deformations on the skirt and the belt buckle.

clothes.name: mindfront_f_dress_05
clothes.author: Mindfront
clothes.description: Tags: Female Elegant. This dress is quite hard to get to deform nicely especially the sleeves Blender's modifier 'Surface Deform' and 'Corrective Smooth' is good help. The base dress is designed by my friend Bellatrix and wanted to share it with MakeHuman community. I am not satisfied with the normal map skirt folds but lesser real folds make it easier to adjust. In Blender use the Solidify modifier with thickness about 1 5mm fill rim and only rim to give the fabric some thickness. I use the slot for displacement for the metal mask texture. The preview image is rendered in Blender Cycles and I used proportional edit sculpt 'Surface Deform' and 'Corrective Smooth' modifier to adjust the dress to fit the character and pose. The fabric looks like it is made of metal but my intention was silk and of some reason appear more pink than intended. The Blender 2.79 material I use can be downloaded here: http://www.makehumancommunity.org/content/f_dress_05_blender_cycles.html

I use the slot for displacement for the metal mask texture. It does not need any subdivision as I accidentally applied one level. In Blender use the Solidify modifier with thickness about 2mm fill rim and only rim to give the fabric some thickness. The preview image is rendered in Blender Cycles and I used proportional edit and sculpt to adjust the dress to fit the character and pose. clothes.name: mindfront_tactical_vest_female
clothes.author: Mindfront
clothes.description: Tags: Work Sport Female. For best fitness I did two version this is best fitted for a female character. You may need to adjust the positions of the zipper slider the shoulder straps and the magazine pockets depending on your character deformations as they use rigid groups and I leave it to you to deform sculpt the pockets so they look more soft and natural as I wanted to keep it as low poly as possible dont know why as it doesn't matter for my purposes . The pockets have each three empty magazine for an AK47 i think and as I did not find any information about their dimension I had to improvise thereof the pockets size but I have seen that some tactical vest pockets are this big. It is ment to be used with solidify modifier thickness about 2 mm and Fill Rim - Only Rim or else the pockets and some details wont look as good. The preview image is rendered in Blender Cycles with the pose 'StandingAtEase' by callharvey3d. As I dont yet have made a fitting jacket and trousers I used my 'Tank Top 01' and 'M Trousers 01' with new textures. On the head is my 'Patrol Cap'.

clothes.name: mtknife_low_mini_skirt
clothes.author: MTKnife
clothes.description: The texture here is from actual fabric. Note that the inside is textured as well as the outside--and that you won't be able to hide faces in MH. UPDATE: I've adjusted the vertex groups on the human proxy and the waist and hem now work for all ages and genders except babies . UPDATE 2: It looks like in the last update I screwed things up by re-uploading some of the files but not others. It's fixed now. UPDATE 3: Added edges between the front and back faces of the skirt to prevent them from separating when a cloth modifier is used. UPDATE 4: Uploaded an mhclo with corrected license and homepage. UPDATE 5: Added tags to the MHCLO file and moved the alternate texture to the 'Materials' section.

clothes.name: mtknife_pleated_mini_skirt
clothes.author: MTKnife
clothes.description: I really wanted to do pleats by creating a mesh in the right shape rather than using normals....It took me close to two years off and on to figure out how to do that. The pleats are in fact folded just like real knife pleats a good guide for making the real thing is https://www.carbonchic.com.au/2014/11/diy-knife-pleated-skirt/ or rather one pleat was folded and then repeated along a bezier curve made from an edge loop in the skirt helper this introduced some distortions in size but they're not horrible . The mesh was designed with quads then transformed to tris using 'poke faces' before being exported as clothes in order to deal with the non-planar faces necessarily created by the folding. The peculiar format of the UV map is also due to folding the mesh like real pleats: to get pixels to end up in the right places the texture needs to be a long strip just like a real pleated skirt is made from a long strip of fabric. Finally note that if you want to do a cloth simulation on the skirt you'll need to do some pinning as the pleats aren't actually attached to the waistband. Credits: In the included material the plaid texture comes from heartlessinu at DeviantArt https://www.deviantart.com/heartlessinu/art/Red-plaid-215324313 while the zipper is from fuzzimo http://www.fuzzimo.com/free-hi-res-zipper-textures-pictures/ . UPDATE: Straightened some of the geometry in the waistband and fixed a few problems that show up on specific models.

clothes.name: punkduck_diver_scuba_rig
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: This diver scuba rig is a co-production of grinsegold who made the geometry and myself. So most of the credits are for him. In Blender you should change the materials a bit. Insert the color output of the texture to the glossy shader. For the silver-like parts you can duplicate the material then delete the fresnel and use a fixed value. In the end you should decrease the roughness of the glossy shader. That's the way the example picture was done. Be aware that parts will deform when changing the geometry or posing the character. You should use this asset with the diver suit. Be aware that these twin hose regulators were used in the 60s and 70s of the last century.

clothes.name: punkduck_diver_suit
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: This diver suit is a co-production of grinsegold who made the geometry and myself. So most of the credits are for him. In Blender you should change the materials a bit. Insert the color output of the texture to the glossy shader. For the weights you can duplicate the material then delete the fresnel and use a fixed value. In the end you should decrease the roughness of the glossy shader. That's the way the example picture was done. Delete groups are used only face and hands are left. Be aware that the weights deform when changing the geometry. An additional material is available. Use this asset with the diver scuba rig.

clothes.name: punkduck_electric_guitar
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: I decided to get Makehuman to the limits and created a e-guitar only with quads and using one texture. Of course in Blender you should change lots of details strings frets etc. to a shiny material. The guitar strap uses normal vertex-groups left/right/mid and partly a special group the guitar itself uses only the special vertex group. Now it scales with the character. You should see that as a demonstration. When you use the subdivision surface algorithm in MH the fretboard texture becomes distorted dots are moving . This does not happen when you use the subdivision surface algorithm of Blender. The example picture is done with the Blender version.

Subdivision surface algorithm in Blender or Makehuman will change the form of the decoration this is intended. The leather litsphere is used in Makehuman to make the normalmap visible. clothes.name: punkduck_halterneck_romper

In the 1970s rompers were usually a casual garment made of terrycloth and often in a tube top style. They were common in the 1980s in a wider variety of materials. Since 2006 rompers have enjoyed a minor renaissance as a fashionable garment for women.' I made a romper with a halterneck. A normalmap is supplied and delete groups are used. Nevertheless it could happen that skin peeks through. Best fit on female characters with small to midsize breasts.

When you import this dress in Blender you should connect the color input of the glossy shader to the image texture. The trumpet sleeves may intersect with the body in certain poses. clothes.name: punkduck_microphone
clothes.author: punkduck

It is built on rigid groups so it should not deform too much. If you want to be totally sure that the microphone does not deform in Blender you can throw away all vertex groups and recreate the metacarpal2.R and metacarpal3.R group. Set the 2.R to 0.35 and the 3.R to 0.65 for all vertices. For the material you can experiment with the roughness of the glossy shader and the ratio between diffuse and glossy shader.

clothes.name: punkduck_one_piece_bikini01
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: This is a one piece bikini. It is colored white and there is a node setup supplied so that you can recolor it e.g. like in 'Baywatch' . There is also advice how to use the shrinkwrap modifier to make it skin-tight. A normal-map is also supplied. Delete-groups are used. Be aware to switch them off when you will work with transparency. There are also two methods to simulate a wet swimsuit one is simply to reduce roughness of the glossy node. There is also a second texture added where transparency is used except where the seams and crotch-area are. This is more 'sexy'. Be aware that for a dark color you need less transparency to make the skin visible. But decide yourself which way you like ...

clothes.name: punkduck_p-ivy_weapon
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: A weapon created by O4saken. I got the permission to upload this weapon into the user contributions to allow users to create a complete Poison Ivy cosplay character. Normalmaps are added and made visible by the leather litsphere in Makehuman. All credits goes to O4saken ... thank you

clothes.name: punkduck_retro_polka_dot_skirt
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: This is a typical 50s skirt with a polka dot pattern. It should be combined with the appropriate retro top. Normal map is added but most of the pleats are directly created by geometry. The pattern should work with subdivision in Blender MakeHuman will distort it a bit. The normal map is made visible by using the leather litsphere. In Blender the litsphere does not matter.

clothes.name: punkduck_retro_top
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: This is a typical 50s top. In reality it is convertible from a square neckline to a off shoulder neckline. It should be combined with the retro polka dot skirt. Normal map is added to create the pleats of the sleeves. The normal map is made visible by using the leather litsphere. In Blender the litsphere does not matter. Delete groups are used.

clothes.name: punkduck_riding_boots
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: The riding boots are designed for female characters and can be combined with the riding breeches. Delete groups are used and a normal map is added.

clothes.name: punkduck_riding_helmet
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: Safety first ... the helmet should be used while riding. It may be combined with riding breeches and boots. A normal map is added.

clothes.name: punkduck_running_shoes_01
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: Typical light-weight running shoes used for long-distance running and triathlon. Delete groups are used.

clothes.name: punkduck_shapewear
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: This colorful shapewear I made for a user request. You should combine it with the black bandeau bra. Deletegroups are used. To create the shiny effects a normal map 4 times as big as the diffuse texture is added. In MH the leather litsphere is used to make the effect visible.

clothes.name: punkduck_shopping_bag01
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: To complete a shopping scene I created one of these gloss coated paper bags. The diffuse-texture containing my logo could be changed to everything you like. The bag is made with rigid groups. I also added a pose to carry this bag and the handbag.

clothes.name: punkduck_skirt_native_american_fashion
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: I created this skirt which is very similar to an existing one made by an native American designer. Not to get into trouble with copyrights I used CC0 feathers scroll and leaves. The glam belt is also made similar to the original. The small spheres may deform unfortunately. You should connect the color input of the glossy shader to the image texture. The buckle in the back may be changed to a silver-like material. For the spheres I changed the white ones to silver and the black ones to a dark glass shader.

clothes.name: punkduck_sleeveless_crop_top
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: This crop top should fit to most of the dresses. This 'initial' version is colored white like most of the t-shirts you can buy so it could be simply changed to a new color or pattern. Delete groups are use. You should connect the input of the glossy shader to the output of the image texture.

clothes.name: punkduck_sleeveless_shirt
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: The sleeveless shirt should be used for female characters. You may combine it with the jeans or minirock. As a material I tried a combination of normal-map and diffuse-texture which works in MH itself. So I took the leather litsphere. When you export it to blender the litsphere does not matter because the materials and textures are added as always. I added a picture in the forum tutorials/coloring white texture... how you change the material in Blender cycles to display every color you want and also how to change the details. The shirt uses the delete-group. All 5 buttons of the shirt resize geometrically Scale down the 4th button when you use bigger cup-sizes in Blender. Select all buttons in Blender and attach a new more shiny material e.g. put a math-node 0.5 clamp between fresnel and mix-shader .

clothes.name: punkduck_spaghetti_strap_tank_top
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: This tank top is designed for the jeans skirt. Maybe you have to make some corrections in Blender because I tried to let it not appear too bulky. When you combine it with the short jeans or tight jeans there IS additional work to do in Blender. As a material I tried a combination of normal-map and diffuse-texture which works in MH itself. So I took the leather litsphere. When you export it to blender the litsphere does not matter because the materials and textures are added as always. I added a picture in the forum tutorials/coloring white texture... how you change the material in Blender cycles to display every color you want. The shirt uses the delete-group.

clothes.name: punkduck_sport-sunglasses
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: This kind of sport sunglasses are used for running and biking. The glasses are completely made by using rigid groups so there may be modifications of the head which will not work with these glasses . In Blender you should change the transparent part of glasses to a transparent/glossy shader combination I did it for the example picture .

clothes.name: punkduck_sport_bra
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: I created a sport bra for female characters which can be also used as a special top for yoga or pole-dance. Best to use this bra with small to mid cup sizes. For bigger cup sizes you should modify the result in Blender. A normal map is added in MakeHuman I use the leather litsphere to make it visible. In Blender the litsphere does not matter. Delete groups are used. Sport briefs are also available. If you want to use the bra as a top you should use the red pole-dance-material.

clothes.name: punkduck_sport_briefs
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: Since it is weird to wear fine lingerie under e.g. a tennis dress I created sport briefs for female characters. Edit: A normal map is added in MakeHuman I use the leather litsphere to make it visible. Delete groups are used. A sport bra is also available. If you want to use these briefs without an additional dress you should use the red pole-dance material.

clothes.name: punkduck_stethoscope
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: This stethoscope was created with new makeclothes v2. You should replace the material where metal is used in Blender. When you want to pose the character be aware that the export from MakeHuman also bends the stethoscope. In this case do an export in normal pose and do changes in Blender directly.

clothes.name: punkduck_strappy_lace_thong
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: This special thong was created by request. It should be only used for young attractive adult females. Obviously it is fully transparent so no delete-groups are used. A normal-map is added but the visibility for the map on a black texture is nearly zero. The texture is a combination of lace-structures put together on different layers using GIMP. If you think it is too erotic simply don't use it. A fitting bralet is also available.

clothes.name: punkduck_string_2
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: String 2 could be used with the transparent bra or the babydoll provided by me. It was made mainly for the babydoll and is not very detailed ...

clothes.name: punkduck_string_4
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: String 4 should be used with the Bandeau bra. It also should fit under the cocktaildress. A white texture will be added in the material section.

clothes.name: punkduck_string_5
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: Sorry for using my enumeration .... String 5 is part of a set the other part is the pushup bra. In Blender using cycles render connect the color input of the glossy shader to the texture and use a value of 0.4 for the mix shader instead of the fresnel input to get the same result shown in the example picture. Delete vertex groups are used.

clothes.name: punkduck_sun_visor_sports_visor
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: From Wiki: This type of headgear was designed for use in outdoor sports especially golf tennis and volleyball where eye protection from direct sunlight is desirable but is now often used by non-athletes at beach and other sunny outdoor events. The strap can function as a sweatband. Of course it is used for running also. It is not so simple to combine it with strand hair in Blender but even if you use it with polygon hair you normally must change geometry. A normal map is supplied. The leather litsphere is used to make the effect visible in Makehuman.

clothes.name: punkduck_swim_cap
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: A typical swim cap which you receive when you want to do a competition. The texture contains the starting number change it if you don't like it ... . The delete groups are used especially to delete part of the ears. The leather litsphere is only used for a visibility of the normalmap. Throw away the hair. The goggles can be used with this cap.

clothes.name: punkduck_teddy_erotic_lingerie
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: This teddy is made for female characters. I used delete_groups so that faces in some regions should not poke through. After exporting this to Blender you normally should edit the distorted rings this will happen when your model has bigger cupsizes and change the material of the rings. You also should make the whole material a little bit more shiny when you use the normal map. An example of the cycles shader setup is placed in the forum.

clothes.name: punkduck_tennis_dress
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: A typical tennis dress for female characters. It should be combined with sport briefs01 for your own security ... : . A litsphere is used to make the normal map visible. Delete-groups are used.

clothes.name: punkduck_tennis_racket
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: I created a tennis racket to complete the scene. You can download a few tennis poses then you will see if it fits. In Blender you may change the frame of the racket to a more glossy material if you want ...

clothes.name: punkduck_tennis_shoes
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: These shoes are derived from my running shoes. I combined the shoes with socks. Delete groups are used. A litsphere is used to make the normal map visible in Makehuman.

clothes.name: punkduck_transparent_bra
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: The transparent bra was made as an alternative to the babydoll.

clothes.name: punkduck_triathlon_suit_female
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: A typical one-piece triathlon suit. You wear this suit when you swim also under the wetsuit bike or run. The suit should be combined with a race number belt. Delete groups are used. The leather litsphere is only used for a visibility of the normalmap in Makehuman. In Blender the litsphere does not matter.

clothes.name: punkduck_tube_dress
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: A rather simple tube dress for female characters. As always you have to rework the breast region after you imported it in Blender when your character has big cupsizes usually by smoothing it. In reality you can buy the dress in 6 different colors. I only add the white material. As a material I tried a combination of normal-map and diffuse-texture which works in MH itself. So I took the leather litsphere. When you export it to blender the litsphere does not matter because the materials and textures are added as always.Please read the small tutorial in the forum for other colors. Delete-Vertex group is used now.

clothes.name: punkduck_tube_top
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: The tube top should work for most female characters. As a material I tried a combination of normal-map and diffuse-texture which works in MH itself. So I took the leather litsphere. When you export it to blender the litsphere does not matter because the materials and textures are added as always. I only add a white diffuse texture. I added a picture in the forum tutorials/coloring white texture... how you change the material in Blender cycles to display every color you want and also how to change the details. The tube top uses the delete-group.

clothes.name: punkduck_v_neck_top
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: This v-neck t-shirt is designed to fit to the tight jeans and short jeans for females. For all of my three models this works fine but it could be that you have to change faces for your models. The v-neck top uses the delete-group. As a material I tried a combination of normal-map and diffuse-texture which works in MH itself. So I took the leather litsphere. When you export it to blender the litsphere does not matter because the materials and textures are added as always. I only add a white diffuse texture. I added a picture in the forum tutorials/coloring white texture... how you change the material in Blender cycles to display every color you want and also how to change the details.

clothes.name: punkduck_wetsuit
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: A wetsuit or neoprene used for swimming competitions or triathlon. It is partly based on Freehunters female bodysuit but also works for males. The delete-groups are used. The strap on the backside uses rigid groups. The leather litsphere is only used for a visibility of the normalmap in Makehuman. It is only used near the armpits ...

clothes.name: punkduck_winter_boots
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: Winter boots with fur stripes for female characters. You may combine it with winter coat the sleeveless shirt and the tight jeans. I only added a diffuse texture the fur itself must be generated with the particle system see description in forum user contributions winter coat . The boots use the delete-group.

clothes.name: punkduck_winter_coat
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: A winter coat with fur stripes for female characters. You may combine it with the sleeveless shirt and the tight jeans. I only added a diffuse texture the fur itself must be generated with the particle system see description in forum user contributions winter coat . The coat uses the delete-group only in the arm and upper body regions. The buckle and part of the belt resize geometrically You may have to push this section up or down when you use smaller or bigger cup-sizes in Blender. Select the buckle and attach a new more shiny material like brass.

clothes.name: punkduck_wireless_headset
clothes.author: punkduck
clothes.description: I added a wireless headset. In times of smartphones and earbuds it might look at bit big ... but that's reality because transmitter and battery are built in . The headset was built using one rigid group. It might disappear in a few hairdoes. Use subdivision of a least 2 in Blender.

clothes.name: thegreatengineer_galactic_warrior_uniform
clothes.author: TheGreatEngineer
clothes.description: This is a uniform I made for one of my characters for a story I'm creating. It's my first upload so don't judge too harshly. Strangely with my Character there seems to be a very slight issue with the weighting as you can see below but for any character scaled reasonably it should work fine. Also this piece is intended for female characters. It's a sci-fi fantasy uniform meant to be worn by soldiers on the battlefield.

clothes.name: toigo_ankle_boots_female
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a pair of ankle boots that will fit most female humans. I have included diffuse specular and normal maps and the .mhmat file should get them all imported into Blender where their properties can be adjusted according to preference and render engine. Illustration shows shoes with subsurf of two and a mean crease of one around the heel and sole edge loops.

clothes.name: toigo_ankle_boots_male
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a pair of ankle boots that will fit most male humans. I have included diffuse specular and normal maps and the .mhmat file should get them all imported into Blender where their properties can be adjusted according to preference and render engine. These boots have the same UV mapping as my Ankle Boots http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/ankle_boots.html . Illustration shows boots with subsurf of two and a mean crease of one around the heel and sole edge loops.

clothes.name: toigo_ballet_flats
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a pair of women's ballet-style flats. They will fit most female humans around ages 15-50 without much adjustment. I have included diffuse specular and normal maps and the .mhmat file should get them all imported into Blender where their properties can be adjusted according to preference and render engine. Illustration shows shoes with subsurf of two and a mean crease of one around the heel edge loops.

clothes.name: toigo_ballet_flats_with_bows
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a pair of women's ballet-style flats with bows. They will fit most female humans around ages 15-50 without much adjustment. I have included diffuse specular and normal maps and the .mhmat file should get them all imported into Blender where their properties can be adjusted according to preference and render engine. Illustration shows shoes with subsurf of two and a mean crease of one around the heel edge loops.

clothes.name: toigo_ballet_flats_with_flowers
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a pair of women's ballet-style flats with flowers. They will fit most female humans around ages 15-50 without much adjustment. I have included diffuse specular and normal maps and the .mhmat file should get them all imported into Blender where their properties can be adjusted according to preference and render engine. Illustration shows shoes with subsurf of two and a mean crease of one around the heel edge loops.

clothes.name: toigo_basic_tucked_t-shirt
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a basic T-shirt with the bottom hemline scaled so that the shirt looks tucked into pants. It was made for adult male humans but will fit other average humans. A subsurf of at least two is recommended. I designed it to be worn with my Wool Pants -- seen in example pic -- http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/wool_pants.html but with a little adjustment it can also be paired with other bottoms. The bump texture is needed for realism. Several other colors are in Materials. Illustration was rendered with subsurf set to two. Model Jerome is also wearing Mindfront's Shoes Monk Strap male http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/shoes_monk_strap_male.html

clothes.name: toigo_bodice-style_top
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a bodice-style top that will fit most female humans. Some slight adjustments may be needed for senior and plus-size humans. I have included diffuse specular and normal maps and the .mhmat file should get them all imported into Blender where their properties can be adjusted according to preference and render engine. Kendra is also wearing my Tiered Skirt Mini http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/tiered_skirt_mini.html

clothes.name: toigo_bodice_dress_with_lace_ruffle_skirt
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a dress with a bodice-style top and a lace ruffle skirt. It was made with the Blender cloth simulator. Illustration shows dress with subsurf of two. A diffuse texture is included. Jacklyn is also wearing my Peeptoe Pumps http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/peeptoe_pumps.html in Natural http://www.makehumancommunity.org/content/peeptoe_pumps_natural.html .

clothes.name: toigo_camisole_dress_with_full_skirt
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a camisole-style dress with a full skirt. It was made made with Blender's cloth simulator. The dress will fit female and mostly female humans but some adjustments may be needed for toddler senior and plus-size humans. A diffuse texture is included. Illustration shows dress with subsurf of two.

clothes.name: toigo_camisole_top
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a camisole-style top that will fit female and mostly female humans. It was made with Blender's cloth simulator. A diffuse texture is included. Illustration shows top with subsurf of two and some slight adjustments were made around the waist to fit the top over the skirt. Kira is also wearing Jeans skirt by punbkduck http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/jeans_skirt.html .

clothes.name: toigo_cut_out_dress
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a dress with cut outs at the waist. It was made with the Blender cloth simulator. Illustration shows dress with subsurf of two. A diffuse texture is included. Jacklyn is also wearing my Flats http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/flats.html in Giraffe print http://www.makehumancommunity.org/content/flats_giraffe.html .

clothes.name: toigo_dress_with_tiered_skirt
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a dress with a tiered skirt. It was made with Blender cloth sim. It will fit most female humans and even some males without much distortion. The neck may need slight adjusting on senior humans. The illustration is toon shaded because that's how I roll these days but the .mhmat file is configured for default shading.

clothes.name: toigo_female_double-breasted_suit
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a women's double-breasted suit that will fit female and mostly female humans. Some adjustments maybe needed for child senior and plus size humans. I have included diffuse specular and normal maps and the .mhmat file should get them all imported into Blender where their properties can be adjusted according to preference and render engine. This suit uses the same UV mapping as my Mens' Double-breasted Suit http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/mens_doublebreasted_suit.html . Illustration shows suit with subsurf of two and some slight adjustments were made to fit the pants over the shoes. Nicole is also wearing my Women's Loafers http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/womens_loafers.html .

clothes.name: toigo_female_suit
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a suit for female humans of almost every body type. Diffuse bump and specular maps are included.

clothes.name: toigo_female_suit_2
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a suit for female humans. It will fit most ages and body types but adjustments will been needed for child senior and plus-size humans. This suit uses the same UV mapping as my Men's Suit 3 http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/mens_suit_3.html . I have included diffuse specular and normal maps and the .mhmat file should get them all imported into Blender where their properties can be adjusted according to preference and render engine. Illustration shows suit with subsurf of two and some slight adjustments were made to the jacket around the crotch area. Nicole is also wearing my Ankle Boots http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/ankle_boots.html .

clothes.name: toigo_fisherman_sweater
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a fisherman knit sweater designed for adult males but will fit other average humans. The texture comes with a normal map which is essential to make the sweater look more sweater-like. Five other colors are available in Materials. Illustration model Hal is also wearing Mindfront's M Trousers 01 which can be found here: http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/m_trousers_01.html

clothes.name: toigo_flats
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: Classic flat shoes for women. Five more colors are available in Materials.

clothes.name: toigo_gloves_long
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a pair of long gloves that will fit most humans. One diffuse texture is included. Illustration shows gloves with subsurf of two.

clothes.name: toigo_gloves_medium
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a pair of medium-length gloves that will fit most humans. One diffuse texture is included. Illustration shows gloves with subsurf of two.

clothes.name: toigo_gloves_short
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a pair of gloves that will fit most humans. One diffuse texture is included. Illustration shows gloves with subsurf of two.

clothes.name: toigo_halter_dress_knee_length
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a knee-length halter dress that was made with Blender cloth sim. One diffuse texture is included. Illustration shows dress with subsurf of two. Whitney is also wearing my Hand Gloves http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/hand_gloves.html and my Heels with Lace Frill Socks http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/heels_with_lace_frill_socks.html in Black and White http://www.makehumancommunity.org/content/heels_with_lace_frill_socks_black_and_white.html .

clothes.name: toigo_halter_dress_midi
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a midi-length halter dress that was made with Blender cloth sim. One diffuse texture is included. Illustration shows dress with subsurf of two. Whitney is also wearing my Medium Gloves http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/medium_gloves.html and my Ballet Flats http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/ballet_flats.html .

clothes.name: toigo_halter_dress_with_fluted_skirt
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a long dress with a fluted skirt. It was made with Blender cloth sim. One diffuse texture is included. Illustration shows dress with subsurf of two and crease 1.0 on the two loops where the skirt is connected to the dress. Whitney is also wearing my Long Gloves http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/long_gloves.html

clothes.name: toigo_harem_pants
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a pair of harem-style pants that were made with Blender's cloth simulator. They will fit female and mostly female humans though some adjustment may be needed for toddler senior and plus-size humans. A diffuse texture is included. Illustration shows pants with subsurf of two. Kira is also wearing Sleeveless Crop Top by punkduck http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/sleeveless_crop_top.html which was color adjusted in Blender Green 1.2 Blue 1.2 Brightness 0.6 .

clothes.name: toigo_keyhole_neck_dress
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a dress with a keyhole neck that will fit most humans but looks best on average adult females. It was made with the Blender cloth sim sewing springs for the fit and regular cloth for cloth-like folds. Recommended modifiers: Solidify .1 in/.25 cm -1 only rim and Subsurf 1 will do but 2 is better . Eight additional materials are available in Materials.

clothes.name: toigo_keyhole_tank_top
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a keyhole style tank top that fits most female humans. It was made using Blender's cloth sewing springs. Recommended modifiers: Solidify .1 in/.25 cm -1 only rim and Subsurf 1 will do but 2 is better . Six more materials are available in Materials. Yvonne is also wearing Tight Jeans by punkduck.

clothes.name: toigo_lace_frill_socks
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a pair of socks with a lace frill. They were designed for adult female humans but will go on almost any other human with little to no adjustment. With proper masking these socks can be used with many different shoe styles.

clothes.name: toigo_leg_warmer_socks
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: These are like the slouchy scrunchy socks that were popular back in the 1980s. There are ten additional colors available in Materials.

clothes.name: toigo_long_fingernails_blue_butterfly
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: These are long fingernails with blue butterfly nail art. Four additional materials can be found in the Materials section of User Contributed Assets. I have included a spec map so the tops are shiny and the undersides dull like natural or sculptured nails usually are. Each nail is 100 faces so they look best with a subdivision surface of two or three. I have updated the mhclo and obj files so the fingernails should work on both hands now.

clothes.name: toigo_long_full_skirt
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a long full skirt that was made with Blender's cloth simulator. It will fit female and mostly female humans though some adjustment may be needed for toddler senior and plus-size humans. A diffuse texture is included. Illustration shows skirt with subsurf of two. Kira is also wearing Sleeveless Crop Top by punkduck http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/sleeveless_crop_top.html which was color adjusted in Blender Red 1.2 Brightness 0.6 .

clothes.name: toigo_maga_hat
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: Nevermind the politics of it. No matter how you feel about Trump you might eventually need a MAGA hat for a character like Uncle Earl who wears his MAGA hat proudly while yelling at the neighborhood kids to get off his lawn.

clothes.name: toigo_male_double-breasted_suit
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a men's double-breasted suit that will fit male and mostly male humans. Some adjustments maybe needed for child senior and big and tall humans. I have included diffuse specular and normal maps and the .mhmat file should get them all imported into Blender where their properties can be adjusted according to preference and render engine. Illustration shows suit with subsurf of two. Owen is also wearing my Men's Loafers http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/mens_loafers.html .

clothes.name: toigo_male_suit_3
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a men's suit with more details than my previous suits. It will fit male and mostly male humans but some adjustments may be needed for child senior and big and tall humans. I have included diffuse specular and normal maps and the .mhmat file should get them all imported into Blender where their properties can be adjusted according to preference and render engine. Illustration shows suit with subsurf of two and some slight adjustments were made to the shirt collar around the neck. Chance is also wearing my Men's Loafers http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/mens_loafers.html .

clothes.name: toigo_male_suit_tie_and_jacket
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a men's suit with a close to universal fit for most male humans. I have included bump and specular maps. Illustration models are also wearing Shoes Oxford male by Mindfront.

clothes.name: toigo_mj_cloth_shoes
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a pair of very low poly shoes 556 vertices 278 per shoe that will fit a wide range of humans but fit best on mostly female humans above age four. Of course the shoes will need a subdivision surface of at least two three or four is even better and creasing on key edge loops -- as shown in the illustration. I have created a forum thread with a more detailed explanation of the edge loops... http://www.makehumancommunity.org/forum/viewtopic.php?f=4&t=16689 I have included diffuse specular and normal maps and the .mhmat file should get them all imported into Blender where their properties can be adjusted according to preference and render engine. Two additional colors are available in Materials.

clothes.name: toigo_round_glasses_leopard
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a pair of round glasses with gradient tint lenses. They were designed for adult female humans but will go on others with just a little scaling of the Y and/or X axes in edit mode. There are seven other colors available in the materials section. Glasses model Sarah is also wearing punkduck's Deathnote T-shirt.

clothes.name: toigo_shift_dress
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a simple shift dress. It was made with the Blender cloth simulator. Illustration shows dress with subsurf of two. A diffuse texture is included. Jacklyn is also wearing my Heels with Lace Frill Socks http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/heels_with_lace_frill_socks.html in Black and White http://www.makehumancommunity.org/content/heels_with_lace_frill_socks_black_and_white.html .

clothes.name: toigo_skirt_with_lace_ruffle
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a lace ruffle trimmed skirt which was made with Blender's cloth simulator. It will fit female and mostly female humans but some adjustments may be needed for toddler senior and plus-size humans. A diffuse texture is included. Illustration shows skirt with subsurf of two. Kira is also wearing Sleeveless Crop Top by punkduck http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/sleeveless_crop_top.html which was color adjusted in Blender Red 1.2 Blue 1.2 Brightness 0.8 Saturation 0.7

clothes.name: toigo_stiletto_booties
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a pair of booties with stiletto heels. They will fit average female -- age weight muscle height and proportions -- humans. I have included diffuse specular and normal maps and the .mhmat file should get them all imported into Blender where their properties can be adjusted according to preference and render engine. Four additional colors are available in Materials.

clothes.name: toigo_strapless_ruffle_top_dress
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a strapless dress with a lace ruffle top and fringe. It was made with the Blender cloth simulator. Illustration shows dress with subsurf of two. A diffuse texture is included. Jacklyn is also wearing my Peeptoe Pumps http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/peeptoe_pumps.html in Floral print http://www.makehumancommunity.org/content/peeptoe_pumps_floral.html .

clothes.name: toigo_suit_with_dinner_jacket
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a men's suit with a dinner jacket that will fit male and mostly male humans. Some adjustments maybe needed for child senior and big and tall humans. I have included diffuse specular and normal maps and the .mhmat file should get them all imported into Blender where their properties can be adjusted according to preference and render engine. Illustration shows suit with subsurf of two and a procedural gold texture was assigned to the trim on the buttons. Owen is also wearing my Ankle Boots Male http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/ankle_boots_male.html in Black http://www.makehumancommunity.org/content/ankle_boots_male_black.html .

clothes.name: toigo_suit_with_jacket_and_bowtie
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a suit with a jacket and bowtie. Bump and spec maps are included. Three alternate colors are available in Materials. Illustration models are also wearing Shoes Oxford Male by Mindfront.

clothes.name: toigo_tiered_mini_skirt
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a mini tiered skirt that will fit most female humans and some males. Some slight adjustments may be needed for toddler senior and plus-size humans. I have included diffuse specular and normal maps and the .mhmat file should get them all imported into Blender where their properties can be adjusted according to preference and render engine. Kendra is also wearing my Bodice-style Top http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/bodicestyle_top.html

clothes.name: toigo_tiered_skirt
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a tiered skirt that was made using Blender's cloth sim. It will fit most female humans and even some males. The waistband may need slight adjusting on toddler senior and/or plus-size humans. There is a Turtleneck Halter Top that goes with the skirt http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/turtleneck_halter_top.html . Both the skirt and top use the same diffuse texture. Six other colors are available in Materials. Yvonne is also wearing my Heels with Lace Frill Socks http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/heels_with_lace_frill_socks.html in White and Pink http://www.makehumancommunity.org/content/heels_with_lace_frill_socks_white_and_pink.html

clothes.name: toigo_turtleneck_halter_top
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is turtleneck halter top which is meant to be matched with my tiered skirt http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/tiered_skirt.html but can be worn with any kind of bottoms or none at all if that's your thing . It will fit most female humans and even some males. The neck area may need slight adjusting on toddler senior and/or plus-size humans. Both the skirt and top use the same diffuse texture. Six other colors are available in Materials.

clothes.name: toigo_wool_pants
clothes.author: MargaretToigo
clothes.description: This is a pair of wool pants designed for male humans but will fit other average humans too. Several other colors are in Materials. Illustration also pictures Shoes Monk Strap male by Mindfront. http://www.makehumancommunity.org/clothes/shoes_monk_strap_male.html

clothes.name: v0rt3x_armsleeves_black_fishnet_large
clothes.author: V0rT3X
clothes.description: Tip : the armsleeves come in 6 different variations. For a better overview you can make it changeable under the Material Tab instead of Clothes. Create therefore just one folder under ../v1/data/clothes/ name it for example armsleeves_fishnet and copy all files of one variation into it. Take for example armsleeves_fishnet_large as the main files. Rename them to armsleeves_fishnet and edit the .mhclo file to match the new names. E.g. rename armsleeves_fishnet_large.obj to armsleeves_fishnet.obj rename armsleeves_fishnet_large.mhmat to armsleeves_fishnet.mhmat etc. Change the new names within the .mhclo files too. IMPORTANT: DO NOT RENAME THE .PNG FILE unless you're willing to edit its name within the .mhmat file too Create then a subfolder named materials and within it create 5 additional subfolders named after your choice. For example: armsleeves_fishnet_medium armsleeves_fishnet_small etc. Now copy only three files of each version the .mhmat the .thumb and the .png into the appropriate subfolder. For example copy armsleeves_fishnet_medium.mhmat armsleeves_fishnet_medium.thumb and armsleeves_fishnet_medium.png into the armsleeves_fishnet_medium subfolder delete the .mhclo and the .obj file. And now the important part rename the .mhmat and the .thumb file to match the name in the main folder so that the main .mhclo file could find it in this case rename it to armsleeves_fishnet.mhmat and armsleeves_fishnet.thumb again leave the .png file unless you're willing to edit it's name within the .mhmat file . Do the same procedure with all 5 subfolders and voila your 6 different armsleeves appear properly under the Material Tab as Armsleeves fishnet. /Tip

IMPORTANT: DO NOT RENAME THE .PNG FILE unless you're willing to edit its name within the .mhmat file too Create then a subfolder named materials and within it create 5 additional subfolders named after your choice. For example: stockings_fishnet_medium stockings_fishnet_small etc. Now copy only three files of each version the .mhmat the .thumb and the .png into the appropriate subfolder. For example copy stockings_fishnet_medium.mhmat stockings_fishnet_medium.thumb and stockings_fishnet_medium.png into the stockings_fishnet_medium subfolder delete the .mhclo and the .obj file. And now the important part rename the .mhmat and the .thumb file to match the name in the main folder so that the main .mhclo file could find it in this case rename it to stockings_fishnet.mhmat and stockings_fishnet.thumb again leave the .png file unless you're willing to edit it's name within the .mhmat file . Do the same procedure with all 5 subfolders and voila your 6 different stockings appear properly under the Material Tab as Stockings fishnet. /Tip

eyebrows.name: mindfront_eyebrows_01
eyebrows.author: Mindfront
eyebrows.description: Mesh high poly eyebrows as an alternative to low poly textured. Instead of using Blender particle eyebrows I decided to make them as mesh to be reusable through MakeHuman. Each hair is non-manifold triangular and goes about 1 mm into the head to avoid visible gaps when posing. Same eyebrows can look awful on some characters and better on other. No texture is needed but they are UV-unwrapped with the roots at the bottom. If you want them darker at the roots and lighter at the tips you can make a BW gradient texture as color mix factor. The preview render is on a default neutral character using subsurf x1 and a simple cycles material.
